title: Neural Networks II — Training
description: How a perceptron learns its own weights: the perceptron learning rule as an iterative algorithm, why convergence is guaranteed exactly when the data is linearly separable, and why stacking perceptrons into layers overcomes what one perceptron alone cannot solve.
objectives: State the perceptron learning rule Δw_i = η(t − y)x_i, Δb = η(t − y) and apply it by hand to a misclassified example
Define an epoch as one full pass over all training examples and describe repeated-epoch training until convergence or a maximum epoch count
Trace several weight updates across multiple epochs by hand for a small linearly separable dataset
State the perceptron convergence theorem: training converges in finite epochs if and only if the data is linearly separable
Explain why training a single perceptron on XOR never converges, using both an algebraic argument and an empirical trace
Explain why stacking perceptrons into a hidden layer lets a network solve problems, such as XOR, that a single perceptron cannot

lessons.title: Training to convergence: linear separability revisited
Beyond one perceptron: why depth helps
